items.title: Historic farm turns Sunday roast into paddock-to-plate dining experience

children.content: A 200-year-old Tasmanian farm is opening its gates to diners for the first time, as the family-run business seeks to expand its offerings and keep on top of costly upkeep.

items.title: Man tied up, house burnt down as thieves steal 9 guns, car

children.content: Emergency services called to a house fire at Tasmania's Coles Bay find the home owner tied up and his home torched after thieves made off with nine guns and his car.

image.altText: Rifles on display at Tasmanian firearms seller.

items.title: Hobart's Mona announces plans to set up art museum in Thailand

children.content: Museum of Old and New Art founder David Walsh signs an agreement to set up MONA Bangkok in Thailand. Mona says the new iteration will be "unconventional and unique", like its Hobart museum.

image.altText: An artist impression of a modern, triangular-shaped building, surrounded by a pond.

items.title: Labor still sliding in Tasmania, despite facing a 12-year-old minority government

children.content: When Labor recorded its lowest electoral result in over a century last year some may have felt the party had hit rock bottom, at least in terms of polling. That may not be entirely the case, writes Lucy MacDonald.

articles.title: Bellingham goes big time again, as England continues down hard road

children.content: Jude Bellingham has been scrutinised and criticised in the months leading up to the World Cup. Now he stands as England's best player so far, carrying the Three Lions' title dreams on his shoulders.

items.title: In this friendship group there are 'no Jews, no Muslims, just one big family'

children.content: When a group of Jewish mates decided to support a newly arrived Muslim family, all of their lives would change. Together they have flipped expectations about religion and friendship.

items.title: Nosková downs 'ex-friend' Muchová to win all-Czech Wimbledon final

items.title: Theoretical 'mirror life' could grow out of control, scientists fear

children.content: This year, scientists included a new potential threat in their decision to move the Doomsday Clock closer to midnight. So what is 'mirror life' and why are they concerned?

items.title: As Japan's population shrinks, 'urban bears' are moving in

children.content: When Araki Osamu first heard reports a bear had been seen in the streets of his home town, a 1-hour bullet train ride north of Tokyo, he dismissed it as "fake news".

items.title: From Heath Ledger protégé to abuse survivor, Grace Woodroofe perseveres

children.content: Heath Ledger launched her music career at just 17, but an emotionally abusive relationship with a fellow musician derailed it. A decade on, Grace Woodroofe is releasing the soundtrack to her survival.

items.title: Time to bite the bullet? Unpopular decision looms over $600m Aboriginal gallery

items.title: Mural celebrates pioneering star behind Australian country hits
